text:
Haven’t seen Paraguay play but are they actually this bad? Seems like they have zero idea how to play against a press, slow to every ball, and general traffic-cone level defending.

Even basic things like defenders moving into space after passing back to the keeper, instead of staying in the shadow of the pressing opponent seems like a foreign concept.
A lot of credit has to go to USA for a great performance, but seems baffling how bad Paraguay played.
label: analysis

text:
Most balanced beatdown I've seen in a while.
Sweden were simply outclassed on the defensive end; the Dutch were able to bypass the midfield on a quick pivot and leverage the skill gap between their own attackers and the Swedish fullbacks. The keeper's reaction time didn't help either.

Netherland's defensive structure seems less stable than previous years, so it will be interesting to see how they hold up as the tournament progresses.
label: analysis

text:
A lot of naivety in Sweden's approach to this match. That back line of 3 with only 1 player in front of then, during defensive transitions, does not work against well balanced teams.

The Netherlands looked like they were playing a videogame, due to how easy it was to attack. They had time, space, and numeric advantage during transitions.
label: analysis

text:
Same pattern as the Brazil game...Strong start, lose momentum, only to not regain it for the whole second half. The only difference is Scotland didn't have enough individual brilliance to make use of the excessive momentum gained.

Morocco may qualify but I fear for them against a good opponent in the latter stages.
label: analysis

text:
I feel like this means Egypt is through.

Next game is against Iran, it they tie they have 5pts. And Iran will have 3pts. If they lose they have 4pts.

If Belgium wins, they'll have 5pts. But if New Zealand wins, then NZ will have 4pts and Belgium will be stuck with 2pts.

So I think they're through now. They have enough points to be top three.
label: analysis

text:
Had Egypt failed to win today, they would have tied Honduras for most World Cup matches without a win.

Instead New Zealand has taken their spot on the list and will match Honduras if they can't beat Belgium

text:
Egypt deserved the game overall and NZ switched off.

That doesn't mean the ref was not atrocious. If anyone sees a compilation of the fouls and yellows given in favour of Egypt and the ones not given to NZ from the first cooling break up till the second Egypt goal, the difference is laughable and consistent.

Not saying he was biased for sure but the game was mostly reffed one-sided.

Pick your favourite.

text:
Spain just look massively different with a winger that's actually trying to beat his man. Also think swapping in Porro for Llorente was a great move, Porro linked up well with Yamal today and really enhanced their threat down the right flank. Real test will be against Uruguay though, Saudi Arabia were woeful.
Also, Ferran Torres defies logic. Comes on, does his routine of missing, somehow manages to score... then has it taken away by VAR. If someone told me Ferran Torres García of Foios, Spain was a scientific experiment to see how a footballer can ragebait watchers I wouldn't even bat an eye at this point.
label: analysis
notes: Borderline: the Ferran section is hyperbolic, but the main claim is supported by named tactical changes and their effects.

text: This exactly. Going out with Gavi and Ferrán as wingers against an 11-man low block was criminal. The verticality of Olmo was sorely missed too. That draw was 100% on De La Fuente (and an amazing defensive/GK effort by Cape Verde).
label: analysis
notes: Borderline: includes a strong verdict, but it names the lineup choice, missing verticality, and opposing defensive performance as evidence.

text:
Obviously, Yamal's starting was a big difference maker, but I think Porro just offers Spain so much dynamism in chance creation down the right side from the fullback position.
I really like the variation between deep crosses, field switches, underlap runs and overlaps he brings to the side when he plays with Yamal.
label: analysis

text:
Japan have historically struggled in game 2 so this is a huge win for us.
One of the reasons Moriyasu picked Nagatomo was for his overall experience sure but mainly his experience in the World Cup. Nagatomo just had a speech in front of the team about the difficulty of playing well in game 2.

While I am sad about missing the injured players like Mitoma, Minamino, Endo, and now Kubo (hopefully he can come back), Moriyasu has finally built a team that he and his team envisioned years ago.
God knows what’ll happen in this tourney but I believe in this team and I believe this team has the ability and team chemistry to win it all.

We don’t back down to anyone.
label: analysis

text:
Its crazy how Kubo looks like a massive miss at first glance yet you look at the XI today and its still full of absolute quality players with multiple more decent options on the bench

They just have a genuinely really good squad

text: I haven’t watched football as technically sound as Japan’s today so far this World Cup. This is the type of teamplay every team should strive for, very little wasted movements and it seems to be very energy conscious too due to how the Japanese players were playing in that Monterrey heat. (As in, they didn’t look that fatigued compared to a team like South Korea for example).
label: analysis

text: Players never complain when they don’t get a pass from their teammate. Selfless running all game. So fun to watch

text: I feel like what makes Japan tough to be beat is because they’re so good technically that they hardly beat themselves. Unlike many teams, if you wanna win against them you need to actually go out and beat them rather than relying on their lapses or mistakes.

text:
Japan's draw is going to be brutal. They'll have to play either Morocco or Brazil and if they somehow lose to Sweden and finish 3rd, they get France.
Brazil/Morocco/France, only options. Meanwhile the USA gets to play Bosnia. Mexico gets Scotland, and Canada will most likely play Iran or Algeria... lol. Not all brackets are created equal.
